q: The magnetic field at the center of a circular loop carrying current I and radius R is:
exp: For a single circular loop, B = μ₀I/2R. Field increases with current and decreases with radius.

q: A charged particle enters perpendicular to a uniform magnetic field. The path followed is:
exp: Magnetic force acts perpendicular to velocity, producing uniform circular motion.

q: The SI unit of magnetic flux is:
exp: Magnetic flux is measured in weber (Wb). 1 Wb = 1 T·m².

q: A long straight conductor carries current upward. At a point east of the wire, magnetic field direction is:
exp: Using right-hand thumb rule, field circles the wire. East side gives downward direction.

q: Force on a current carrying conductor in magnetic field is maximum when angle between current and field is:
exp: F = BIL sinθ. Maximum force occurs when sinθ = 1.

q: The magnetic field inside an ideal solenoid is:
exp: Inside a long solenoid, field lines are parallel and equally spaced, giving nearly uniform magnetic field.

q: The force on a moving charge q in magnetic field B is given by:
exp: Lorentz magnetic force depends on charge, speed, field strength and angle.

q: A proton and an electron enter same magnetic field with same speed perpendicular to field. Which experiences greater force magnitude?
exp: F = qvB. Magnitude depends on charge magnitude, which is same for both.

q: The radius of circular path of charged particle in magnetic field is proportional to:
exp: r = mv/qB, so radius increases with speed.

q: Magnetic field due to a long straight wire at distance r is:
exp: From Biot-Savart/Ampere’s law, field decreases inversely with distance.

q: Two parallel wires carrying current in same direction:
exp: Parallel currents in same direction attract due to magnetic interaction.

q: The torque on a current loop in magnetic field is maximum when plane of loop is:
exp: Torque τ = nBIA sinθ where θ is angle between normal and field. Maximum when normal is perpendicular to field.

q: The SI unit of magnetic field is:
exp: Tesla measures magnetic flux density.

q: A current carrying conductor placed parallel to magnetic field experiences:
exp: F = BIL sinθ. For θ = 0°, force is zero.

q: The magnetic field at center of a circular coil increases if:
exp: B = μ₀NI/2R. More turns strengthen magnetic field.

q: A positively charged particle moving north enters magnetic field vertically upward. Direction of force is:
exp: Using Fleming’s left-hand/right-hand rule or vector product v × B, force acts east.

q: Ampere’s circuital law relates magnetic field with:
exp: ∮B·dl = μ₀I_enclosed.

q: The magnetic moment of a current loop depends on:
exp: Magnetic moment m = NIA.

q: In a cyclotron, charged particles gain energy due to:
exp: Magnetic field bends path while electric field accelerates particles.

q: The magnetic field lines around a straight conductor are:
exp: Field forms concentric circles around conductor.

q: Assertion (A): Magnetic force can change direction of velocity of charged particle. Reason (R): Magnetic force acts perpendicular to velocity.
opts: Both A and R are true and R explains A
Both A and R are true but R does not explain A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
exp: Perpendicular force changes direction but not speed, causing circular motion.

q: A wire carries 5 A current. Magnetic field at 0.2 m from wire is approximately:
A charged particle moving parallel to magnetic field experiences:
exp: Magnetic force depends on sinθ. Here θ = 0°.

q: The direction of magnetic field inside a solenoid is determined by:
exp: Curl fingers along current; thumb gives field direction.

q: If current through a wire doubles, magnetic field around it becomes:
exp: Magnetic field is directly proportional to current.

q: The force between two parallel conductors forms the basis of definition of:
exp: Ampere was historically defined using force between current carrying conductors.

q: The work done by magnetic field on moving charge is:
exp: Magnetic force is perpendicular to displacement, so no work is done.

q: The path of charged particle entering magnetic field at an angle is generally:
exp: Velocity component parallel to field remains unchanged, perpendicular component causes circular motion.

q: A toroid is preferred over solenoid because:
exp: Magnetic field remains confined inside toroid.

q: Magnetic flux through a surface is maximum when magnetic field is:
exp: Φ = BA cosθ, maximum for θ = 0° with area vector.

q: The field at the center of a circular coil with N turns is:
exp: Each turn contributes equally to magnetic field.

q: Which device uses magnetic effect of current?
exp: Motor works due to force on current carrying conductor in magnetic field.

q: The magnetic field inside a toroid depends on:
opts: Current and turns per unit length
exp: B = μ₀NI/2πr.

q: A current carrying loop behaves like:
exp: Current loop produces dipole magnetic field similar to bar magnet.

q: The radius of cyclotron motion decreases if:
exp: r = mv/qB. Stronger magnetic field tightens circular path.

q: Magnetic field lines never intersect because:
opts: Two directions of field at same point are impossible
exp: Intersection would imply two magnetic field directions at one point.

q: A rectangular loop carrying current is placed in uniform magnetic field. Net force on loop is:
exp: Opposite forces cancel, though torque may exist.

q: Which quantity remains unchanged when charged particle moves in magnetic field only?
exp: Magnetic field changes direction of motion, not speed.

q: Magnetic field due to straight conductor decreases with distance as:
exp: Inverse distance dependence from Ampere’s law.

q: An electron moving east enters magnetic field northward. Direction of force is:
exp: For positive charge force is downward, electron being negative experiences upward force.

q: The magnetic field at the axis of a circular loop is strongest at:
exp: Field magnitude is maximum at loop center.

q: The direction of force on conductor carrying current in magnetic field is determined by:
exp: Thumb gives force direction, forefinger field, middle finger current.

q: If magnetic flux linked with a surface is zero, the magnetic field must be:
exp: Flux depends on perpendicular component of field.

q: A proton enters magnetic field and moves in circle of radius r. If speed doubles, new radius becomes:
exp: r ∝ v for fixed q and B.

q: Magnetic effect of current was first discovered by:
exp: Oersted observed compass needle deflection near current carrying wire.

q: A wire bent into circle carries current. If radius halves keeping current same, field at center becomes:
exp: B ∝ 1/R, so halving radius doubles field.

q: Magnetic field inside an infinitely long solenoid is independent of:
exp: Inside ideal solenoid, field is uniform everywhere.

q: The magnetic force on stationary charge is:
exp: F = q(v × B). If v = 0, force vanishes.

q: Assertion (A): Magnetic field can change direction of momentum of charged particle. Reason (R): Magnetic force is always perpendicular to velocity.
opts: Both A and R are true and R explains A
Both A and R are true but R does not explain A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
exp: Perpendicular magnetic force continuously changes momentum direction without changing magnitude.
